content.full_text:
FPPC

APR 23 10 04 AM '90

April 19,1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street      Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

ATTN:  MARGARET ELLISON

Dear Margaret,

As per our telephone conversation on April 19, 1990, I am re-
questing written confirmation on use of funds in a political
account to be used for air fare to Sacramento in conjunction
with an advocacy program with the East San Gabriel Valley
Republican Women and the City of Glendora.

My political account was established for the purpose of my
re-election to the Glendora City Council.  I was defeated on
April 10th; however, there are remaining funds in the account.
As Legislative Chair of the East San Gabriel Valley Republican
Women Federated, while a City Councilmember, I agreed to go
to Sacramento, as I have in the past, to participate in the
Advocacy Program of the East San Gabriel Valley Republican Women
Federated on May 1 & 2.  I feel it important to fulfill my
obligations and commitment to the Republican Women and City of
Glendora.  It has been my practice to share equally the information received from attending the program with both organizations.

It was your opinion over the phone, if I understood correctly,
that these funds would not be surplus until the end of the re-
porting period, and that the expenditure of those funds for prior
commitments would be appropriate.  I would request that written
confirmation for the use of these funds for the stated purpose be
sent immediately.


Sincerely yours,

[signature]
Rose M. Shade
811 East Juanita
Glendora, Ca.  91740
818-963-3895


1

[The image appears to be a very faint or low-contrast document. Most of the text is illegible or too faint to read accurately. Only fragments of text are barely visible at the top of the page, but they are too unclear to transcribe with confidence.]
